transcription_notes: Transcribed from the UCT 2027 Undergraduate Prospectus faculty admission tables (Commerce p27-30, Engineering p36-38, Health p43-45, Humanities p55-58, Law p62, Science p69-70). Scope: undergraduate degrees across all 6 faculties; Humanities diplomas/certificates (Music Performance, Theatre & Performance, ACET) and Health higher-certificate/advanced-diploma deferred. scoring_method = uct_fps (percentage-sum APS/600; Science doubles Maths + Physical Sciences /800; Health adds NBT /900). min_aps = each programme's Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS. Health programmes are gated on the documented sub-minimum APS (out of 600) because the /900 FPS needs NBT scores not captured by UniFlo; WPS (disadvantage/redress) and NBT performance levels are non-computable and captured in notes. UCT publishes no programme codes here so qualification_code is null. Technical Mathematics/Technical Science are NOT accepted as substitutes for Mathematics/Physical Sciences.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Commerce (BCom)
programmes.notes: All applicants must write the NBT (Academic & Quantitative Literacy). Most BCom specialisations. min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Business Science (BBusSc)
programmes.notes: All applicants must write the NBT (Academic & Quantitative Literacy). Most BBusSc specialisations. min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: BCom / BBusSc: Computer Science, Statistics & Data Science
programmes.notes: All applicants must write the NBT (Academic & Quantitative Literacy). BBusSc stream is 4 years. min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Business Science: Actuarial Science / Quantitative Finance
programmes.notes: All applicants must write the NBT (Academic & Quantitative Literacy). English FAL route requires Proficient AL & QL NBT scores. min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Science in Engineering: Chemical Engineering
programmes.notes: NBTs (Maths, AL, QL) must be written but are not used for admission. min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Architectural Studies (BAS)
programmes.notes: Requires a creative portfolio (Band A portfolio score 75%+) and possible interview. NBTs (Maths, AL, QL) must be written but are not used for admission. min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Medicine & Bachelor of Surgery (MBChB)
programmes.notes: Also requires 70% for the next three best subjects (excl LO). Selection uses a Composite Index (FPS out of 900 = APS + NBT). NBT not captured here, so this gates on the documented sub-minimum APS; actual selection is highly competitive by FPS/WPS band.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Science in Physiotherapy
programmes.notes: Plus 60% for the next three best subjects (excl LO). Selection uses a Composite Index (FPS out of 900 = APS + NBT). NBT not captured here, so this gates on the documented sub-minimum APS; actual selection is highly competitive by FPS/WPS band.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts (BA)
programmes.notes: NBT Academic Literacy required (Quantitative Literacy for quantitative majors). min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Social Science (BSocSc)
programmes.notes: NBT Academic Literacy required (Quantitative Literacy for quantitative majors). min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Social Science in Philosophy, Politics and Economics (PPE)
programmes.notes: NBT Academic Literacy required (Quantitative Literacy for quantitative majors). NBT QL Upper Intermediate. min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Social Work (BSW)
programmes.notes: Max 80 places; may require an admissions interview. NBT Academic Literacy required (Quantitative Literacy for quantitative majors). min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts in Fine Art (BA(FA))
programmes.notes: Leading indicator is the portfolio evaluation; places awarded on merit. NBT Academic Literacy required (Quantitative Literacy for quantitative majors).

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts in Theatre & Performance (BA(T&P))
programmes.notes: Leading indicator is the audition; places awarded on merit. NBT Academic Literacy required (Quantitative Literacy for quantitative majors).

programmes.name: Bachelor of Music (BMus)
programmes.notes: Audition, interview and music theory test required (leading indicator). NSC Music 60% or Unisa Theory Gr V / Practical Gr VII. NBT Academic Literacy required (Quantitative Literacy for quantitative majors).

programmes.name: Bachelor of Laws (LLB)
programmes.notes: Undergraduate 4-year LLB. NBT AL Proficient and QL Intermediate+ required. International applicants: FPS 510+. min_aps is the Band A 'guaranteed admission' FPS; applicants below it may still receive offers via the weighted WPS (redress) or EDU bands.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Science (BSc)
programmes.notes: FPS out of 800 (Maths + Physical Sciences doubled); min FPS 550 (Band C), 660 Band A. NBTs written for placement, not admission. Where Physical Sciences was not taken, Information Technology or Life Sciences may be doubled for certain majors only. UCT admits to the single BSc degree; majors are chosen within it (some have first-year capacity selection). Majors: Applied Mathematics, Applied Statistics, Archaeology, Artificial Intelligence, Astrophysics, Biochemistry, Biology, Chemistry, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Business Computing, Environmental & Geographical Science, Genetics, Geology, Human Anatomy & Physiology, Marine Biology, Mathematics, Mathematical Statistics, Ocean & Atmosphere Science, Physics, Quantitative Biology, Statistics & Data Science.
